nodes.summary: The article analyzes how PSD (Social Democratic Party) has lost its traditional electorate due to perceived corruption and elite opulence, while PNL and USR have isolated PSD within the governing coalition by firmly supporting Prime Minister Ilie Bolojan. Sociologist Barbu Mateescu explains that PSD's lost voters migrated to Călin Georgescu and AUR, as disillusioned citizens from neglected urban peripheries and rural areas reject PSD's material handouts in favor of anti-corruption messaging.
talking_points: PSD has been isolated within the coalition after PNL and USR firmly backed PM Bolojan and rejected PSD's demands to replace him
PSD lost its captive electorate because voters are disgusted by the ostentatious wealth and corruption of PSD elites like Grindeanu and Ciolacu
Corruption concerns matter more to frustrated voters than price increases, and PSD cannot credibly address corruption since it is part of the problem
AUR humiliated PSD during the budget vote, and the lost PSD electorate may be captured by a new extremist party created before the next elections
The pro-European reformist electorate hopes Bolojan will lead a credible reform alliance for the 2028 elections
nodes.author: Rodica Culcer

nodes.label: Între cerul prostiei și văzduhul insultei
nodes.summary: Cristian Preda criticizes the dual plague of Romanian politics: the emphatic stupidity of sovereignist figures like Călin Georgescu, who speaks in mystical nonsense, and the aggressive insults from PSD politicians like Adrian Câciu and Victor Ponta. He argues that moderate, educated citizens are caught between these two forces and finds personal refuge in culture and intellectual life.
talking_points: Călin Georgescu's nonsensical response about the US-Israel-Iran conflict reveals the depth of sovereignist absurdity in Romanian politics
PSD politicians like Adrian Câciu use ethnic slurs and personal insults in parliamentary committees, degrading political discourse
Victor Ponta's outburst against USR members, calling them 'non-humans' and 'antichrists', exemplifies toxic political rhetoric
Romanian moderates are trapped between sovereignist nonsense and PSD aggression with no clear political refuge
The author's personal solution is retreat into culture and intellectual community, which he admits is a form of egoism
nodes.author: Cristian Preda

nodes.label: La moartea unui mare ziar
nodes.summary: The article examines the deliberate destruction of The Washington Post by Jeff Bezos as a symptom of a broader crisis in free press, drawing parallels with Romania's post-1990 media decline. The author argues that political capture of newsrooms, social media algorithms that amplify rage, and audience tribalization have eroded democratic discourse and eliminated reference media in both the US and Romania.
talking_points: Jeff Bezos is deliberately undermining The Washington Post, a paper that once brought down President Nixon and exemplified press as the fourth estate
Romania experienced similar media capture after 1990, with newspaper owners becoming politically compromised through tax evasion blackmail or financial incentives
Social media algorithms reward fury over truth, radicalizing audiences who no longer need or trust professional journalists
The loss of shared narratives and consensus on basic facts has tribalized even compassion and suffering
Press deregulation and partisan capture transformed journalists from democracy's watchdogs into establishment guard dogs
nodes.author: Doina Popescu

nodes.label: Lumea de Ieri, Lumea de Azi și Lumea de Mâine
nodes.summary: The article analyzes Ursula von der Leyen's controversial speech declaring Europe can no longer be the guardian of the old world order, drawing parallels with Stefan Zweig's 'The World of Yesterday' to argue that Europe must reinvent itself rather than cling to nostalgia or abandon its values. It critiques both von der Leyen's apparent willingness to discard values along with outdated forms, and her critics who defend abstract principles without proposing concrete solutions for a changed geopolitical reality.
talking_points: Von der Leyen declared Europe can no longer guard the old world order and that EU unanimity in foreign policy is an obstacle to geopolitical credibility
Critics like Antonio Costa and Teresa Ribera pushed back, defending multilateralism and international law as fundamental premises
The parallel with Stefan Zweig illustrates the danger of European intellectuals refusing to engage with messy political realities while clinging to abstract principles
The rules-based international order is already in clinical death: US-Israel bombing Iran without UN mandate, China ignoring international courts, Russia invading Ukraine with limited consequences
Europe must do what Schuman and Monnet did in 1950 — pour old values into completely new forms rather than choosing between abandoning values or clinging to obsolete structures
nodes.author: Andrei Colțea

nodes.label: Make Europe Great Again – How Trump Succeeds in Federalizing the EU and What We Must Do
nodes.summary: The article analyzes how Trump's chaotic foreign policy inadvertently created an opportunity for EU federalization, as evidenced by Ursula von der Leyen's speech promoting European independence. It argues Europe must urgently pursue energy independence, new trade alliances (MERCOSUR, India, ASEAN countries), unified legislation, data protectionism, and security integration to become a truly independent geopolitical power.
talking_points: Von der Leyen seized the opportunity created by Trump's erratic behavior to advance the EU federalization agenda, promoting European independence in energy, security, and technology
Europe must build new trade alliances beyond the US-China bipolar framework, including MERCOSUR, India, Canada, UK reintegration, and individual ASEAN member states
A unified EU legislative framework, integrated financial markets, and data protectionism are essential for competitiveness and AI innovation
Energy union and security union would complete de facto federalization, with institutional reform being the final formal step
Romania's leadership is criticized as spineless and unwilling to take any meaningful geopolitical position
nodes.author: Andrei Colțea

nodes.label: Make PSD Great Again?!
nodes.summary: The article draws parallels between Romania's PSD party and Trump's MAGA movement, criticizing PSD leaders for eagerly meeting with Paolo Zampolli, a dubious Trump envoy, and for adopting destructive anti-European political tactics. It argues that PSD's campaign against PM Bolojan and its illiberal tendencies mirror Trump's scorched-earth approach, threatening Romania's EU credibility and democratic values.
talking_points: PSD officials enthusiastically met with Paolo Zampolli, Trump's dubious 'special envoy for global partnerships,' who has ties to Jeffrey Epstein and questionable business dealings
PSD is driving an irresponsible anti-Bolojan political campaign that threatens Romania's credibility with Brussels and international rating agencies
The party's illiberal instincts align it more with Trump's MAGA movement than with the pro-European coalition it nominally belongs to
President Nicușor Dan is vulnerable to PSD blackmail due to the unresolved doctored photos scandal involving Ana Maria Geană and the incomplete report on the 2024 election annulment
Romania's pro-European coalition tolerates UDMR's active support for Orbán's FIDESZ, an agent of Trump and Putin, without challenge
nodes.author: Rodica Culcer

nodes.label: Mitologia populistă și falimentul economic și politic
nodes.summary: The article analyzes PSD's assault on Prime Minister Bolojan as part of a broader system of political spoliation maintained since 1989, now facing bankruptcy. It argues that PSD's double game of agreeing to corrective budget measures while publicly opposing them threatens Romania's OECD accession and international credibility, while extremist parties like AUR serve as controlled opposition created by intelligence services.
talking_points: PSD, AUR, and intelligence services form an integrated system designed to maintain a post-1989 regime of privileges and undeserved spoliation now approaching collapse
PSD's strategy is to let PNL's Bolojan take political blame for painful fiscal corrections while publicly opposing the very measures they agreed to in coalition
Romania's fiscal revenues at 27% of GDP are far below the EU average of 40%, and OECD experts recommend real fiscal reform
Removing Bolojan would risk Romania's OECD accession, credit rating downgrade to junk status, and PSD's own political suicide given its declining poll numbers (16% support)
Rational criticism of extremist leaders Simion and Georgescu is futile because it only preaches to the converted and fails to reach their actual electorate
nodes.author: Rodica Culcer

nodes.label: The European Cultural Model and the New World Order
nodes.summary: The article argues that Europe's strength lies in its 2,000-year-old cultural and democratic heritage, rooted in Greek and Roman civilization. Facing a new world order marked by authoritarian leaders in Russia, America, and China, the author contends that small countries like Romania can only thrive through EU unity, and calls for a European army and an executive EU presidency to respond rapidly to crises.
talking_points: The world has undergone a deep, permanent, and systemic rupture since 2020, not merely a transition
Sovereigntist arguments are fundamentally flawed because no small European country can negotiate alone with superpowers like Russia, China, or the USA
Europe needs to create a unified military force and an executive EU presidency capable of rapid crisis decision-making
Rising extremism across Europe is directly linked to the authoritarian model promoted by Russian and American leaders
The European democratic model, rooted in 2,000 years of civilization, has always prevailed over authoritarianism in the long run
nodes.author: Adrian Nița

nodes.label: Mor în continuare români de Covid. Statul a sistat importul de vaccinuri și ne recomandă să ne spălăm pe mâini
nodes.summary: Romania has completely stopped importing COVID-19 vaccines, becoming the only EU country where vaccination against COVID-19 is unavailable to the population. Despite ongoing infections and deaths, the anti-vaccine movement fueled by sovereignist politicians has also caused measles vaccination rates to drop to 47.4%, leading to a declared national epidemic with 30 deaths between 2023-2025.
talking_points: Romania is the only EU member state where COVID-19 vaccination is completely unavailable to the public, with the government ceasing vaccine imports
The anti-vaccine movement, amplified by sovereignist politicians like George Simion and Diana Șoșoacă, has caused general vaccination rates to plummet, including measles coverage falling to 47.4%
Over 40,000 Romanians tested positive for COVID-19 in 2025 alone, yet vaccines, updated annually in other countries, cannot be obtained anywhere in Romania
Citizens with chronic conditions or repeated COVID infections are forced to consider traveling abroad for vaccination at their own expense
The pandemic exposed deep institutional failures: over 67,000 COVID deaths, GDP contraction of 3.7%, and widened educational and social inequalities
nodes.author: Vlad Ilina

nodes.label: Mulțumim, Ucraina!
nodes.summary: The article argues that Ukraine's resistance against Russia has significantly weakened Moscow's ability to support its allies globally, particularly Iran. It suggests that Trump's aerial campaign against Iran was only possible because Russia, exhausted by the war in Ukraine, can no longer project power or assist Tehran, while China quietly benefits from Russia's economic depletion.
talking_points: Ukraine's resistance has trapped Putin in his own chaos strategy, paralyzing Russia's global military and diplomatic reach
Russia's silence during the Iranian crisis demonstrates its inability to support allies, which should concern other Moscow partners
Trump's campaign against Iran would likely not have been launched if Russia retained its pre-war capacity to intervene
China is patiently exploiting Russia's economic exhaustion, potentially recovering disputed territories like Vladivostok and Sakhalin without military action
Neither Trump nor Netanyahu are likely to acknowledge their strategic debt to Ukraine's sacrifices
nodes.author: Desk Russie

nodes.label: Noi, medicii, vrem să profesăm în România. Dar se intră pe pile și sistemul e absurd
nodes.summary: A Romanian medical student near graduation describes the systemic failures of Romania's healthcare and medical education system, including nepotism in hiring, inadequate residency training, poor pay, and brain drain. The author proposes concrete reforms such as transparent national hiring competitions, better pay for residents, and facilitating diploma equivalence for doctors trained abroad.
talking_points: Medical residency positions in major Romanian cities are effectively reserved through nepotism and connections, leaving qualified graduates without fair access
Romanian medical residents earn 3,500-6,000 lei/month net while working full-time with only 1 of 4-5 monthly on-call shifts paid, pushing some to take second jobs
The old guard of senior doctors deliberately limits residents' hands-on training to protect their own patient base, forcing young doctors to seek real training abroad
Doctors who train abroad face bureaucratic obstacles to returning, including slow diploma equivalence and impossible entry into public hospitals
The author proposes national transparent hiring competitions, increased residency positions, better guard pay, and organized international training stages
nodes.author: Alexia Petrovai

nodes.label: Noii patrioți: guralivi, slabi de minte, mincinoși și ipocriți
nodes.summary: The article criticizes the manufactured outrage by self-proclaimed 'patriots' over President Nicușor Dan's military plane being delayed at Paris Bourget airport due to weather conditions. A pseudo-journalist falsely claimed Swiss F-16s intercepted the presidential flight rather than escorting it as a gesture of gratitude, forcing Dan to release air traffic control recordings to counter disinformation. The author argues this fake patriotic hysteria overshadowed the actual purpose of the Paris trip — supporting Ukraine.
talking_points: Weather delays at Bourget airport were exploited by nationalist voices to manufacture a fake scandal about presidential dignity
A pseudo-journalist falsely claimed Swiss F-16s were intercepting rather than honoring the Romanian presidential flight, forcing the president to release ATC recordings
The manufactured controversy completely overshadowed the actual substance of the Paris meeting on supporting Ukraine
President Dan announced plans to create a counter-disinformation department at Cotroceni palace
The meaning of 'patriot' has been corrupted — once referring to those who served their country, now describing loud, dishonest hypocrites
nodes.author: Cristian Preda

nodes.label: Noua Ordine Mondiala: Lumea Multipolară
nodes.summary: The article analyzes the emerging multipolar world order, declared effectively dead at the 2026 Munich Security Conference. It examines how major powers — the US, Russia, China, and the EU — are reconfiguring their spheres of influence through energy politics, military positioning, and economic expansion, while Europe struggles to define its strategic role in this new era.
talking_points: The post-1945 world order has been declared dead by major leaders at the Munich Security Conference, with Merz, Macron, and Rubio all acknowledging a new geopolitical era
The US is consolidating influence in the Western Hemisphere through energy politics in Venezuela and strategic Arctic positioning in Greenland
Russia maintains military relevance through its China partnership and Arctic cooperation, while leveraging its war-mobilized economy in Ukraine
China pursues predominantly economic global expansion through infrastructure investments, especially in Africa, while maintaining pressure on Taiwan
The EU remains caught between normative ambition and strategic limitations, perceived as reactive rather than decisive in the new power competition
nodes.author: Mihnea Macovei

nodes.label: "It's not about Ukraine, but about the world order" – The Axis of Autocrats prepares Digital Dystopia 2.0
nodes.summary: The article reviews Anne Applebaum's recent works on 'Autocracy 2.0' – hybrid regimes that maintain democratic facades while functioning as soft dictatorships, leveraging digital technologies and social media. It examines how autocratic states (China, Iran, Belarus, North Korea) collaborate to support Russia's war in Ukraine, forming an 'Axis of Autocrats' that threatens the post-1945 international order. Applebaum calls for a democratic coalition of civic activists and governments to counter authoritarian cooperation.
talking_points: Autocracy 2.0 regimes maintain democratic appearances through facade elections and controlled media while functioning as soft dictatorships
Unlike 20th century totalitarianism, modern autocracies use social media troll farms and media smear campaigns instead of mass repression
The war in Ukraine reveals a coordinated network of pariah states (China, Iran, Belarus, North Korea) supporting Russia with impunity
Sergei Lavrov's call for a 'new world order' signals autocrats' intent to use all means – corruption, media control, state assassinations – to change post-1945 borders
Applebaum advocates for a coalition of democrats – civic activists cooperating with democratic governments – to counter the autocratic axis
nodes.author: Alexandru Gabor

nodes.label: A Look Towards the Hungarian Elections
nodes.summary: The article examines Hungary's obstructionist role within the EU, tracing Viktor Orbán's pro-Russian, Eurasian-oriented foreign policy back to Hungary's historical revisionism rooted in the Treaty of Trianon. It analyzes how Orbán has positioned Hungary at the intersection of Trumpism, Putinism, and Sinophilia, while upcoming elections could see the opposition TISZA party led by Péter Magyar challenge his grip on power.
talking_points: Hungary blocked the EU's €90 billion loan to Ukraine at the March 2026 European Council, demanding Russian oil supplies as a precondition
Orbán's pro-Russian stance is rooted in Hungary's historical revisionism stemming from the Treaty of Trianon and its loss of two-thirds of its territory
Hungary's 95% dependence on Russian gas and the no-bid Rosatom nuclear contract fundamentally shape its foreign policy alignment
Orbán has cultivated a unique geopolitical position at the crossroads of Trumpism, Putinism, Chinese investment, and even pan-Turkic ideology
Opposition party TISZA, led by MEP Péter Magyar, may defeat Orbán according to some polls
nodes.author: Desk Russie

nodes.label: Ormuz: poarta unui zeu, vulnerabilitatea unei lumi
nodes.summary: The article traces the Strait of Hormuz from its ancient Persian origins through colonial conquests to the 2026 crisis, when US-Israeli strikes on Iran triggered a de facto blockade that collapsed global shipping insurance and spiked oil prices. Iran selectively allowed passage only to 'friendly' nations (China, Russia, India), reinventing medieval toll-gate practices and exposing the fragility of global supply chains far beyond oil—including fertilizers, aluminum, and helium.
talking_points: The Strait of Hormuz is not just an oil chokepoint but the critical artery for global fertilizer, aluminum, helium, and petrochemical supply chains with no alternative pipeline infrastructure

Iran's 2026 selective blockade created a two-tier transit system, charging fees in Chinese yuan and only permitting passage to 'friendly' nations, violating UN Convention on the Law of the Sea Article 38
The real weapon was not military but financial—maritime insurers cancelled war-risk coverage within 48 hours, European banks refused commodity credits, and Brent crude spiked to $126/barrel
NATO allies refused Trump's request to send warships to secure the strait, exposing a crisis of multilateral cooperation
Historical pattern repeats: from Portuguese conquest in 1507 to British blockade in 1951 to Iran's 2026 toll system, whoever controls the strait controls global commerce
nodes.author: Diana Florentina Năsulea

nodes.label: Ortodoxismul românesc ca o armă
nodes.summary: The article distinguishes between Orthodox Christianity (ortodoxia) and its political instrumentalization (ortodoxismul), arguing that Romanian politicians weaponize religious identity to promote exclusionary nationalism. Drawing parallels between national-Christianity and national-socialism, the author warns that Orthodox symbols are being co-opted to discriminate against non-Orthodox Romanians and to deliver masked political messages under theological guise.
talking_points: Orthodoxy as authentic Eastern Christian practice must be distinguished from 'orthodoxism' — its cynical political instrumentalization stripped of ethics and morality
National-Christianity shares structural similarities with national-socialism: both construct a superior social group and use identity markers (religion or race) as discrimination criteria
Romanian politicians exploit Orthodox identity to frame policy debates on education, immigration, abortion, and fiscal policy as defense of the Christian nation against Western decadence
Church figures violate the Byzantine principle of church-state 'symphony' by taking political positions, delivering disguised political guidance under theological cover

The ideology begins subtly in education — claiming apostolic Christianization of Romanians by Saint Andrew — and escalates to dividing citizens into 'good' (Orthodox) and 'bad' (agnostic, atheist, other faiths)
nodes.author: Andrei Ghitea

nodes.label: Participarea Președintelui la Consiliul de Pace al lui Donald Trump – opinii pro și contra
nodes.summary: The article compiles diverse opinions on Romanian President Nicușor Dan's decision to attend Donald Trump's Board of Peace summit in Washington as an observer. Analysts debate whether this was a pragmatic move driven by national security interests or a misguided step that places Romania in embarrassing company alongside Hungary's Viktor Orbán. The discussion touches on Romania's lack of preparedness for concrete peacekeeping contributions and the broader tension between realism and moral principles in foreign policy.
talking_points: Romania's geographic proximity to the Ukraine war theater and dependence on US military infrastructure (Deveselu shield, American troops) justify pragmatic engagement with the Trump administration
Romania coordinated its observer participation with European partners including Italy, Cyprus (EU presidency holder), and the European Commission, not acting unilaterally
Romania came to the Board of Peace without any concrete military or peacekeeping contribution offer, unlike Italy which proposed carabinieri troops
Critics argue the observer status places Romania in embarrassing company with Hungary's Orbán and signals accommodationism rather than principled foreign policy
Supporters contend that Romania cannot afford to antagonize the US regardless of who leads it, given direct Russian threats at its border
nodes.author: Redacția

nodes.label: Four Questions for PSD Members and Not Only for Them
nodes.summary: Cristian Preda critiques PSD leader Sorin Grindeanu's proposal for a new internal party consultation, arguing the questions PSD poses resemble those of an opposition party despite being in government. Preda proposes four alternative questions he considers more important for Romanian citizens, challenging PSD's nearly 14-year grip on governance and Grindeanu's controversial political record.
talking_points: PSD launched a new internal consultation before finishing the previous one announced in December, revealing organizational dysfunction
The party's proposed questions—whether to keep PM Bolojan and whether USR should remain in government—are opposition-style questions from a governing party
PSD's electoral base is a small minority: only 19% in presidential elections and 22% in legislative elections with 52% turnout
After nearly 14 years in government producing five prime ministers (Ponta, Grindeanu, Tudose, Dăncilă, Ciolacu), the author argues PSD should move to opposition
Grindeanu's previous tenure as PM was marked by OUG 13, which triggered the largest street protests in Romania's political history
nodes.author: Cristian Preda

nodes.label: PNL s-a comportat ca un partid socialist sub guvernarea Nicu-Marcel. Poate redeveni liberal prin 'userizare'
nodes.summary: The article critiques Romania's National Liberal Party (PNL) for abandoning fiscal conservatism under the Iohannis-Ciucă leadership, adopting socialist-style spending while maintaining poor tax collection. It argues Romania has created an unsustainable hybrid model with Scandinavian-level social spending on a developing-country revenue base, risking a Greek-style sovereign debt crisis.
talking_points: PNL transformed from a fiscally conservative party into a vehicle for budgetary populism under the Iohannis-Ciucă tandem
Romania adopted a toxic hybrid model: Scandinavian-level social spending with banana-republic-level tax collection, creating an unsustainable fiscal gap
The 'investment' narrative used by PNL-PSD to justify record deficits actually masks political clientelism and vote-buying through public spending
The Anghel Saligny program (50 billion lei without real budget coverage) exemplifies clientelist allocation vs. the reform-based PNRR model that USR supported
The concept of 'USR-ization' of PNL is reframed positively as a return to genuine liberal principles of fiscal responsibility
nodes.author: Redacția

nodes.label: Romania's Outdated and Kitsch Foreign Policy Visits Washington
nodes.summary: The article criticizes Romania's diplomatic delegation to Washington, led by Foreign Minister Oana Toiu and including controversial figure Titus Corlățean, arguing that Romania's foreign policy is outdated and naively aligned with a declining MAGA movement. The author contends that Romania should pivot toward European partnerships rather than clinging to a US strategic partnership that effectively no longer exists under the current administration, while also criticizing President Nicușor Dan's lack of leadership and transparency regarding foreign interference in Romanian elections.
talking_points: Romania's Washington delegation is pursuing an outdated diplomatic strategy by courting MAGA figures who will soon become a historical mistake
The US-Romania strategic partnership effectively no longer exists under MAGA leadership, and Romania should rely on European partners for security rather than NATO under current conditions
President Nicușor Dan lacks leadership and transparency, avoiding clear positions to maintain neo-USL coalition support while failing to address foreign interference in Romanian elections

The US House Judiciary Committee's pseudo-report on EU interference in Romanian elections represents American hypocrisy, given the US's own democratic deficiencies with tech giants and uncontrolled lobbying
Any association with MAGA will bring only medium and long-term damage to Romania's interests
nodes.author: Andrei Colțea

nodes.label: Bulgaria's President Resigned to Enter 'Dirty' Politics Arena. What's Next for the Neighboring Country?
nodes.summary: Bulgarian President Rumen Radev resigned in January 2026 to enter active politics, leaving the country in an institutional vacuum while it paradoxically achieves strategic goals like eurozone accession. His departure triggered intense polarization, with supporters seeing him as a patriotic reformer and critics viewing him as another oligarchic figure disguised in anti-corruption rhetoric.
talking_points: Bulgaria operates on 'autopilot' without an approved budget, with a caretaker government, and key institutional pillars either decapitated or delegitimized
Radev's resignation discourse centers on a 'new social contract' and criticism of 'facade democracy' where oligarchic structures control real power outside official institutions
Radev personalizes the oligarchic model around Delian Peevski while himself maintaining ties to influential business circles and security establishment
The anti-corruption rhetoric aligns with urban middle-class parties but Radev's motivations appear rooted in deep state factional disputes rather than genuine institutional reform
Bulgaria's institutional crisis coexists with achieving strategic objectives like Schengen and eurozone entry, creating an ironic sense that the state functions through inertia
nodes.author: Vladimir Mitev

nodes.label: Problema Coaliției este chiar "COALIȚIA"
nodes.summary: Former coalition participant Ionuț Moșteanu argues that the institutionalization of weekly coalition meetings undermines effective governance. He contends that once a prime minister and cabinet receive parliamentary investiture, party leaders should not micromanage executive decisions, and cites the 2021 Anghel Saligny program under Florin Cîțu as an example of poor coalition-driven fiscal decisions.
talking_points: Weekly coalition leadership meetings create a para-constitutional decision-making body that lacks executive mandate
Once a government receives parliamentary investiture, party leaders should let ministers govern according to the agreed program
The coalition as institution should only intervene for major directional changes, not routine governance
The 2021 Anghel Saligny local investment program (10 billion euros) was a fiscally irresponsible coalition decision that undermined EU recovery fund implementation
Government performance should be measured by implementation of the governance program, with censure motions as the accountability mechanism
nodes.author: Ionuț Moşteanu

nodes.label: Problema politicii americane față de Venezuela
nodes.summary: Francis Fukuyama analyzes Trump's limited 'nation building' strategy in Venezuela, arguing that his purely extractive approach focused on oil wealth cannot succeed without establishing a legitimate democratic government. Drawing parallels to failed U.S. interventions in Afghanistan and Iraq, Fukuyama contends that even minimal objectives like restoring oil production require political stability, democratic legitimacy, and the return of millions of Venezuelan emigrants.
talking_points: Trump's Venezuela strategy is cynically limited to extracting oil wealth while ignoring democratic restoration, sending a dangerous signal of old-fashioned imperialism
Even the minimalist goal of restoring Venezuelan oil production cannot be achieved without a legitimate government, security, and the return of skilled professionals
Venezuela's massive diaspora of nearly 8 million people represents both a regional burden and an essential resource that will only return under democratic conditions
Unlike Afghanistan and Iraq, Venezuela has a strong democratic tradition, suggesting that free elections supervised by the OAS could provide a path to legitimacy
U.S. oil companies are reluctant to reinvest due to political instability and a history of expropriation under previous regimes
nodes.author: Francis Fukuyama

nodes.label: Protests in Iran – Hope for a Civil State and Return to Democracy
nodes.summary: The article traces Iran's democratic traditions from the 1905 Constitutional Revolution through the Mosaddegh era, arguing that the 1953 CIA-MI6 coup destroyed secular opposition and paved the way for theocracy. The author watches current Iranian protests with hope that the country might recover its interrupted constitutional and parliamentary heritage rather than settle for monarchy or softened Islamism.
talking_points: Iran had a genuine constitutional and parliamentary tradition dating back to 1905-1911, with rule of law concepts, civil rights, and separation of powers
PM Mosaddegh's democratic government was overthrown in 1953 by CIA and MI6 primarily to control Iranian oil, replacing democracy with authoritarian monarchy
The Shah's secret police SAVAK destroyed secular and democratic opposition, leaving religious clergy as the only organized opposition force
The 1979 Islamic Revolution was an ironic consequence of Western intervention that eliminated all non-religious opposition
Current protests represent hope for recovering Iran's pre-1953 democratic traditions rather than accepting monarchy or reformed Islamism
nodes.author: Doina Popescu

nodes.label: Putem fi oare optimiști?
nodes.summary: The article analyzes whether Romania's reformist camp has reason for optimism after the Bolojan government reduced the financial deficit beyond targets. It argues that PSD systematically sabotages reform efforts, blocks fiscal adjustment measures, and sustains a corrupt 'conglomerate' of political factions, secret services, and judiciary elements that threatens Romania's European future.
talking_points: The Bolojan government's deficit reduction to 7.7% (vs planned 8.4%) sparked cautious optimism among pro-European reformists
PSD acts as a systematic saboteur of political stability and fiscal reform, blocking spending rationalization measures and generating public scandals
A powerful 'conglomerate' of political factions, secret services, and judiciary (especially CCR and ÎCCJ) threatens democratic governance and must be dismantled
Justice reform is stalled - the Lia Savonea network strengthens daily, magistrate pension reform appears lost, and the CCR can block any legislative change
President Nicușor Dan's weak political position without a party base limits his ability to drive intelligence service reform and support the reformist agenda
nodes.author: Rodica Culcer

nodes.label: Putinismul este popular în serviciile secrete românești? – o explicație
nodes.summary: The article analyzes why Putin's model of governance is attractive to Romanian intelligence officers, based on insights from Russia expert Armand Goșu. It argues that Russian intelligence services under Putin achieved a historical first by capturing the state entirely, and Romanian secret service officers envy this status rather than adhering to any ideological alignment with Russia.
talking_points: Under Putin, Russian intelligence services achieved an unprecedented historical shift from being servants of the state to becoming owners of the state
Romanian intelligence officers admire the Putin model not out of ideological sympathy but out of professional envy for the unchecked power and wealth their Russian counterparts enjoy
In democracies, intelligence officers face parliamentary oversight, budget constraints, and media scrutiny, which they find frustrating
The Călin Georgescu operation is framed as intelligence services producing 'one of their own' as a political figure
The attraction is described as a caste-based professional admiration rather than pro-Russian ideology
nodes.author: Redacția

nodes.label: Evil is absolute, good is relative in Romanian politics
nodes.summary: The article argues that Romanian democracy is endangered because corrupt political forces unite effectively (absolute evil) while well-intentioned citizens remain divided by their own critical standards (relative good). The author identifies lack of education and critical thinking as the root cause of vulnerability to propaganda, referencing the 2024 presidential election social media manipulation, and proposes USR as a potential reformist solution before the 2028 elections.
talking_points: Romanian democracy is threatened by united corrupt political forces that exploit poor education and emotional manipulation of neglected populations
The 2024 presidential elections exposed Romania's vulnerability to social media propaganda, and the effects persist even outside electoral periods
Critical thinking, while essential against manipulation, paradoxically divides well-intentioned citizens who hold each other to impossibly high standards
USR could lead a reformist government by 2028 if it reunites former members and returns to meritocratic principles, potentially exceeding 22% as in 2019 EU elections
Without reform, Romania faces a dark period under AUR and PSD with retrograde policies, similar to Trump's rapid transformation of the US
nodes.author: Florin Burta

nodes.label: Recesiune politică severă
nodes.summary: Cristian Preda argues that Romania's most severe recession is political, not economic. The governing coalition is paralyzed by internal disputes, delayed appointments, and tax increases, while President Nicușor Dan risks repeating Klaus Iohannis's pattern of indolence and disconnection from voters.
talking_points: The economic recession debate is a distraction from the deeper political recession undermining Romanian democracy
The four-party coalition (PSD, PNL, USR, UDMR) has only managed consensus on raising taxes, while failing on reforms and key appointments
PM Bolojan has publicly expressed frustration with his mandate, while PSD figures like Grindeanu criticize their own government more harshly than the opposition
President Nicușor Dan's indolence and denial of coalition dysfunction echoes the detachment that characterized Klaus Iohannis's presidency
In functional democracies, coalition agreements are detailed and binding — Romania's coalition operates in the opposite manner
nodes.author: Cristian Preda

nodes.label: Recuperarea identității moldovene – cum tratează scriitorii contemporani din R. Moldova amintirile din comunism
nodes.summary: The article examines how contemporary Moldovan writers process Soviet-era trauma through literature, focusing on identity recovery and collective memory. It analyzes works by authors like Vladimir Beșleagă, Emilian Galaicu-Păun, and Dumitru Crudu, who explore linguistic, ethnic, and social identity struggles under the Soviet system. Their prose transforms personal and collective memories of totalitarianism into a literature of post-totalitarian recovery.
talking_points: Contemporary Moldovan novelists continue to thematize the struggle for linguistic, social, and ethnic identity within the Soviet system, forming a literature of post-totalitarian recovery

Soviet policies including deportations, famine, collectivization, Russification, and cultural isolation left deep scars on Moldovan collective memory that writers are still processing three decades later
The literary return to childhood is one of the most resonant and common creative strategies in contemporary Moldovan writing, serving as an act of identity reconstruction
Authors like Beșleagă pioneered resistance to socialist realism in the 1960s by transforming war experience into interior dramas of conscience and identity crisis

Writers explore how children growing up in Soviet families internalized contradictions between official ideology and authentic values, with small acts of rebellion representing fragile exercises in freedom
nodes.author: Oxana Gherman

nodes.label: Renașterea libertății: Istoria Societății Mont Pèlerin. Interviu cu Stefan Kolev
nodes.summary: An interview with economist Stefan Kolev about the history of the Mont Pèlerin Society (MPS), tracing its origins from the 1938 Walter Lippmann Colloquium through its 1947 founding, and examining how liberal intellectuals sought to reinvent liberalism after its perceived failures in the 1930s. The article explores internal ideological tensions between neoliberals and paleo-liberals, the society's shift toward American-style economics under Friedman's generation, and whether a similar intellectual revival could happen today.
talking_points: The Mont Pèlerin Society and the Walter Lippmann Colloquium were critical responses to liberalism's perceived failure in the 1930s, gathering roughly 50% of all self-declared liberal intellectuals of that era

The society experienced a fundamental shift from broad ordoliberal thinking integrating social, political, and cultural dimensions to a narrower American-style economic liberalism under Friedman and Stigler
MPS's greatest success was having a ready-made policy program when Keynesianism failed in the 1970s, enabling direct influence on Reagan and Thatcher
A revival of MPS-style energy would require both acknowledgment of recent failures and a new positive liberal program that is radical in substance but empathetic in language
nodes.author: Redacția

nodes.label: Restaurația
nodes.summary: The article criticizes PSD Justice Minister Radu Marinescu's controversial nominations for top prosecutor positions, particularly Cristina Chiriac for Prosecutor General, who has a record of hiding evidence and failing to finalize corruption cases. It questions whether President Nicușor Dan will have the political courage to reject these nominations, arguing that as an independent president without solid parliamentary support, he is effectively captive to the entrenched political system.
talking_points: Justice Minister Marinescu strategically mixed scandalous and acceptable nominations to push politically obedient prosecutors into top positions
Cristina Chiriac, nominated for Prosecutor General, hid evidence in a pedophilia case and obstructed an EPPO fraud investigation involving a PSD politician
President Nicușor Dan faces his first real test of political courage but lacks the parliamentary support needed to confront the entrenched system
The traditional coalition parties (PSD, PNL, UDMR) are fundamentally unreformable and deeply connected to security services
Romania is described as a captured state where only minor administrative adjustments are possible, not fundamental reform
nodes.author: Rodica Culcer

nodes.label: Revenirea la bunul simț bugetar
nodes.summary: The article defends Prime Minister Bolojan's budget adjustment policies against two types of criticism: those who say austerity is unnecessary and those who advocate for spending cuts paired with tax reductions. Using macroeconomic data from 2015-2024, the author argues that Romania's unsustainable growth model based on low taxes and high spending had to end, and that the current modest consumption reduction is a small price to pay compared to a Greek-style crisis.
talking_points: Romania's 2015-2024 economic model of low taxes and high spending created an unsustainable fiscal deficit reaching 9.3% of GDP in 2024
Nearly 90% of Romania's fiscal revenues cover only rigid expenditures (salaries, social assistance, and debt servicing), leaving almost no room for discretionary spending
Romania had among the lowest tax rates in the EU across consumption, labor, and property taxes, making revenue increases necessary
The cumulative individual consumption growth of 29.27% over 2015-2024 means the projected 0.1% decline in 2025 is a minimal correction, not a catastrophe
A purely expenditure-cutting approach without tax increases would be politically impossible and economically counterproductive
nodes.author: Victor Giosan

nodes.label: Se pregătește oare a treia suspendare?
nodes.summary: The article discusses the possibility of a third presidential suspension in Romania, triggered by a legal challenge from AUR lawyer Uscov against Constitutional Court judge Dacian Dragoș's qualifications. Author Cristian Preda argues that Dragoș's legal teaching experience at a political science faculty fully qualifies as 'higher legal education,' and warns that AUR leader Simion is using this legal maneuver as a stepping stone to suspend President Nicușor Dan.
talking_points: AUR lawyer Uscov challenged CCR judge Dacian Dragoș's appointment, claiming his teaching experience in a political science faculty doesn't count as legal education

Preda argues that constitutional law and other legal disciplines are integral to political science faculties, citing jurist Iulia Motoc who taught exclusively at such a faculty before serving at CCR, ECHR, and ICC
AUR has already collected signatures to initiate a third presidential suspension and would need half of PSD's parliamentary votes to succeed
The political calculation relies on a Court of Appeals ruling against Dragoș, which would delegitimize President Nicușor Dan's appointment process
PSD could use a favorable court ruling as political cover to join the suspension effort while claiming to defend rule of law
nodes.author: Cristian Preda

nodes.label: Sindromul Gigel Paraschiv sau de ce se găsește greu un ministru al Educației
nodes.summary: Political scientist Cristian Preda critiques the chronic dysfunction in Romania's education system, centered on the 14-year tenure of state secretary Gigel Paraschiv, who symbolizes institutional tolerance of rector power abuses and plagiarism. Preda argues that PM Bolojan's government has started poorly on education reform, appointing compromised figures to key advisory positions.
talking_points: Gigel Paraschiv has served as state secretary for education for nearly 14 years continuously, enabling rector abuses and plagiarism
University rectors illegally extend their mandates beyond the legal two-term limit, with some reaching four or five terms
The current Bolojan government has appointed compromised figures like Marilen Pirtea, Ligia Deca, and Sorin Costreie to education-related positions
Romania's university budget allocation is incomparably smaller than other EU member states, intensifying internal power struggles over scarce resources
True education reform will only begin when Paraschiv is replaced and the cycle of power abuse and plagiarism defense ends
nodes.author: Cristian Preda

nodes.label: "The Immoral Solution": What It Was and What It No Longer Is
nodes.summary: The article analyzes how Romanian presidents have historically positioned themselves when forced into coalitions with political adversaries, coining it 'the immoral solution.' It critiques current President Nicușor Dan for failing to maintain political independence from PSD, contrasting his passive neutrality with Băsescu's transparent self-criticism and Iohannis's initial anti-PSD stance.
talking_points: Băsescu coined the 'immoral solution' in 2004 to transparently acknowledge his uncomfortable coalition with PUR/Dan Voiculescu's party, maintaining voter trust through honesty
Iohannis initially adopted a confrontational anti-PSD stance but eventually capitulated through the 'governmental rotary' power-sharing arrangement
President Nicușor Dan failed to support his own Foreign Minister Oana Țoiu in the Dubai repatriation scandal involving ex-PM Ponta
Dan's condescending treatment of Cotroceni protesters opposing PSD justice minister nominations suggests alignment with PSD rather than neutrality
Romanian political culture is built on confrontation rather than rational compromise, making neutrality a risky bet that can alienate supporters without winning opponents
nodes.author: Cristian Preda

nodes.label: Subsidy for weeds: why does Romania pay millions for bare land? We prefer to treat, not to prevent
nodes.summary: The article criticizes Romania's agricultural subsidy system, which rewards land ownership rather than actual production, allowing landowners to collect EU funds without generating any agricultural output. It argues for raising eligibility thresholds, investing in food processing infrastructure, replacing post-disaster compensation with insurance systems, and fiscalizing EU funds above €5,000 to ensure accountability and competitiveness.
talking_points: Romania's per-hectare subsidy system rewards land ownership equally whether the land produces crops or weeds, undermining productive farmers
Romania remains trapped as a raw material exporter, selling cheap grain and importing expensive processed food products
Post-drought compensation keeps farmers dependent on government aid instead of building resilience through insurance and irrigation systems
EU funds exceeding €5,000 should be fiscalized to ensure accountability and productive use of hundreds of millions of euros
The eligibility threshold for subsidies should increase from 1 hectare to at least 3-5 hectares to filter out non-productive recipients
nodes.author: Sebastian Cernic

nodes.label: Suntem fan-tas-tici!
nodes.summary: A satirical commentary on Romanian President Nicușor Dan's attendance at Donald Trump's first Board of Peace meeting, where Trump called Romanians 'fantastic' while mistakenly referring to Dan as prime minister. The article mocks various Romanian political factions' reactions to the event, from sovereignists claiming the gaffe was intentional, to enthusiastic supporters celebrating improved US-Romania relations, to critics concerned about Romania distancing itself from European allies.
talking_points: Trump called Nicușor Dan 'fantastic' but mistakenly referred to him as prime minister, sparking varied reactions across Romania's political spectrum
Romanian sovereignists and TikTok supporters of Călin Georgescu interpreted Trump's gaffe as intentional recognition of Georgescu as the 'real' leader
Some commentators celebrated Dan's Washington trip as neutralizing the threat from Simion, Georgescu, and Șoșoacă, while others noted Romania's marginalized seating position
Critics raised concerns about Romania distancing itself from France, UK, and Germany while aligning with the US, and questioned Romania offering justice expertise for Palestine
The article satirizes the universal self-congratulation across all Romanian political camps following the Washington visit
nodes.author: Cristian Preda

nodes.label: Suntem în 1939. Ce face Nicușor Dan?
nodes.summary: The article draws parallels between Romania's current security situation and the precarious position it faced in 1939-1940, when its alliance system collapsed rapidly. It warns that US support under MAGA/Trump is unreliable and urges President Nicușor Dan to keep Romania firmly aligned with European allies rather than drifting toward the MAGA-Putin axis.
talking_points: Romania's current security situation is compared to 1939, when the country had firm British and French guarantees that quickly proved worthless after Poland and France fell
The US under Trump/MAGA is portrayed as an unreliable security guarantor whose interests align with Moscow, echoing how France failed Romania in 1940
President Nicușor Dan is urged to prioritize European alignment and participate in shaping the EU's new security architecture
Romania's interwar political elite ignored warning signs while enriching themselves, a pattern the author implies could repeat
The old security system Romania relied upon has effectively disappeared and a new one must be urgently sought
nodes.author: Matei Udrea

nodes.label: Sovereignty on Debt: How Romania Paid Off Its External Debt and What It Lost in Return (1978–1989)
nodes.summary: The article examines Romania's decision under Ceaușescu to rapidly pay off its entire external debt between 1981 and 1989, analyzing the severe economic and social costs imposed on the population. While the debt was formally eliminated by April 1989, the forced austerity—food rationing, energy cuts, import restrictions—eroded living standards, technological capacity, and regime legitimacy, ultimately contributing to the 1989 revolution.
talking_points: Romania's external debt grew rapidly in the late 1970s due to energy-intensive socialist industrialization and reliance on imported technology, reaching over 10 billion USD by 1981
Ceaușescu chose total rapid debt repayment over gradual adjustment, driven by geopolitical motivations to eliminate any external leverage over Romania
The regime implemented simultaneous austerity measures: severe import cuts, forced exports, food rationing, and energy restrictions that devastated daily life
Despite cutting most imports, Romania maintained costly crude oil imports (~2 billion USD/year) because its heavy industry depended on them, revealing a structural energy dependency
The debt was officially paid off by April 1989, but the accumulated 'internal debt'—impoverishment, public health crises, and loss of legitimacy—led directly to the regime's collapse
nodes.author: Robert Băluță

nodes.label: Țara surâsului prezidențial
nodes.summary: The article criticizes Romanian President Nicușor Dan for his lack of foreign policy expertise, his absence from the Davos Economic Forum, and his ambiguous positioning between the US and EU on issues like Greenland and trade tariffs. Author Rodica Culcer argues that Dan's presidential advisor Marius Lazurcă attempts to justify contradictory diplomatic positions, particularly the claim that Romania is 'solidary with both antagonistic positions' of the US and EU.
talking_points: Nicușor Dan's absence from Davos is symptomatic of Romania's diplomatic irrelevance, not a strategic choice
Presidential advisor Lazurcă's claim that Romania is 'solidary with both antagonistic positions' of the US and EU is intellectually incoherent
Dan's Facebook post on the Greenland crisis signals a distancing from the EU and alignment with Washington, as identified by Oxford professor Corneliu Bjola

The president fails to defend the rule of law domestically, with unresolved issues around the CCR blockage on magistrates' special pensions and the aftermath of Russian interference in the 2024 elections
Dan appears to be manipulated by shadowy figures like Marius Lazurcă, who has shown anti-Ukrainian sympathies
nodes.author: Rodica Culcer

nodes.label: Chaos Theory, from Bucharest to Washington, via Tehran
nodes.summary: The article argues that political decisions in Romania, the US, and partly the EU are not driven by strategic vision but by absolute confusion and short-term instincts. It criticizes Romania's hesitation to join France's nuclear deterrence initiative, the chaotic Romanian coalition politics, and the US military strikes on Iran conducted without congressional authorization or clear objectives.
talking_points: Romanian coalition politics are driven by petty electoral advantages rather than strategic governance or addressing the budget deficit
Romania is making a critical mistake by hesitating to join France's European nuclear deterrence initiative, clinging to an outdated illusion of guaranteed American protection through NATO
Defense Minister Miruță demonstrates political invertebracy by refusing to take clear positions on Ceaușescu's legacy and on European nuclear cooperation
The US strikes on Iran were launched without congressional declaration of war, without defined objectives, and without an exit strategy, mirroring the same chaos theory pattern
European security architecture is rapidly evolving and Romania risks being excluded by its indecisiveness
nodes.author: Andrei Colțea

nodes.label: The Paper Tiger in the Jungle of a Multipolar World
nodes.summary: The article argues that NATO has become a 'paper tiger' as Trump himself declared, and that Europe must shift its paradigm to view the US as a geopolitical rival rather than a protector. The author contends that whether Europe intervenes in the Strait of Hormuz crisis or not, it must do so for the right reasons—building autonomous security, energy, and military capabilities rather than clinging to an outdated transatlantic framework.
talking_points: NATO was always transactional—the US provided security in exchange for political solidarity and recognition of American geopolitical supremacy, but this strategic convergence no longer exists
Europe must operate in a new paradigm where the US is a geopolitical rival alongside China and Russia, not a paternalistic protector
Any European intervention in the Strait of Hormuz should be motivated by protecting economic interests and building autonomous capabilities, not by preserving an obsolete security framework
The EU is already building its multipolar position through trade agreements with MERCOSUR, India, and Australia, and discussions about UK rejoining the EU signal a new direction
Europe should continue supporting Ukraine and integrate it into a new European security architecture where Russia is a manageable threat and NATO is optional
nodes.author: Andrei Colțea

nodes.label: Trump nu a încheiat opt războaie. Ce spune minciuna asta despre el și lume
nodes.summary: The article debunks Trump's repeated claim of having ended eight wars, analyzing each supposed conflict resolution (Thailand-Cambodia, Kosovo-Serbia, India-Pakistan, Egypt-Ethiopia, Congo-Rwanda) and showing they were either trivial, pre-existing, or illusory. The author argues Trump's behavior reflects narcissistic personality traits worsened by cognitive decline, comparing his shameless overcompensation to the dignified confusion of ordinary people facing dementia.
talking_points: Trump's claim of ending eight wars is systematically debunked - most were minor skirmishes, pre-existing resolutions, or fabricated peace deals
Trump's cognitive decline is masked by his narcissism and total lack of shame, unlike ordinary people who experience dementia with dignity
The India-Pakistan mediation backfired strategically by pushing India toward China, undoing decades of American diplomacy
Trump's SMS to Norway's PM linking the Nobel Prize to Groenland demands reveals irrational behavior that cannot be explained by any rational geopolitical framework
The article connects Trump's foreign policy failures to Romanian figures like Potra and Georgescu through mercenary operations in Congo
nodes.author: Cristian Ghinea

nodes.label: Trump Has Just Demolished the Last Vestiges of the Old World Order. It's Time for a United Europe
nodes.summary: The article argues that Trump's illegal intervention in Venezuela signals the collapse of the post-WWII international order, effectively giving Russia and China a green light to pursue their own spheres of influence. The author makes an urgent case for European federalization, arguing that only a united EU with a federal government can survive in a world where the US, Russia, and China all threaten European interests.
talking_points: Trump's intervention in Venezuela mirrors 19th-century colonial behavior and undermines the rules-based international order, emboldening Russia and China
Major powers are reverting to spheres of influence: the US claims the Americas and Greenland, China claims the Western Pacific, Russia claims Eastern Europe
Europe's only chance for survival is political unification into a federation or confederation with a federal government managing strategic domains
The EU's combined GDP of $22 trillion makes it competitive with the US ($29T) and far exceeds China ($18T), but only if it acts as one entity
The US under Trump has officially classified Europe as an adversary in its new security strategy, joining Russia and China as threats to European existence
nodes.author: Matei Udrea

nodes.label: Ucraina – supraviețuire prin europenizare
nodes.summary: The article argues that Ukraine's Europeanization has evolved from a diplomatic project into an existential survival mechanism against Russian imperial revisionism. It traces Ukraine's European trajectory from the Orange Revolution through the 2022 invasion, showing how war transformed EU integration from a policy goal into a component of national resilience, with over 170 billion euros mobilized by the EU between 2022-2025 and public support for EU membership reaching 85.5%.
talking_points: Ukraine's EU integration is not an emotional wartime reaction but a historical return to a cultural and political space it has always partially belonged to
The war eliminated strategic ambiguity and made multi-vector foreign policy impossible, turning Europeanization into a national survival mechanism
EU reforms—independent judiciary, anti-corruption, civil control of the military—now function as elements of wartime resilience rather than mere accession conditions
The EU has undergone a paradigm shift, mobilizing over 170 billion euros and treating Ukraine as a future member state whose stability is a European public good
EU enlargement can no longer be treated as a purely technical-bureaucratic process; it is now inseparable from European security policy
nodes.author: Nicolae Țîbrigan

nodes.label: Ultima zi (sper) fără o ministră sau un ministru al educației
nodes.summary: The author, an education advocate, reflects on the eve of a new education minister's appointment, criticizing the coalition's failed reforms and rector-dominated negotiations that ignored pre-university education. She calls for depoliticization of education and a meaningful Education Pact backed by an independent mechanism with real experts.
talking_points: The coalition's education negotiations were dominated by university rectors seeking more autonomy, while pre-university education was ignored
Summer non-reforms aged the teaching corps dramatically, increased simultaneous teaching class sizes, and demotivated students and experienced teachers who saw salary cuts
The current coalition represents a window of opportunity for a real Education Pact, but only if backed by a functional and independent mechanism staffed with experts
Electoral campaigns are no longer won by mobilizing teachers in party-colored jackets but through TikTok, making this the right time for depoliticization
Poverty deepens without proper education reform, and poverty is the enemy of education
nodes.author: Corina Atanasiu

nodes.label: Small State Diplomacy In Great Power Competition
